author: xsacha
text: Vulkan: error -5 when trying to use it with Nvidia card

author: xsacha
text:
## 🐛 Bug

When attempting to use Vulkan backend, it failed with error -5 (VK\_ERROR\_MEMORY\_MAP\_FAILED)

## To Reproduce

$ python3 -c 'import torch; x = torch.randn(1,3,112,112); x = x.to("vulkan")'  
Traceback (most recent call last):  
File "", line 1, in   
RuntimeError: VkResult:-5

It fails specifically with these messages with VK\_DEBUG\_ALL enabled:  
DEBUG: Loading layer library libVkLayer\_MESA\_device\_select.so  
INFO: Failed to find vkGetDeviceProcAddr in layer libVkLayer\_MESA\_device\_select.so  
DEBUG: Unloading layer library libVkLayer\_MESA\_device\_select.so

Also, my original image I had attempted failed with -2 (VK\_ERROR\_OUT\_OF\_DEVICE\_MEMORY) even though the size did not seem unreasonable.

$ python3 -c 'import torch; x = torch.randn(8,3,1024,768); x = x.to("vulkan")'  
Traceback (most recent call last):  
File "", line 1, in   
RuntimeError: VkResult:-2

## Expected behavior

It works, I suppose.

## Environment

* PyTorch Version (e.g., 1.0): 1.10.0a0+gitdb11619
* OS (e.g., Linux): Linux
* How you installed PyTorch (`conda`, `pip`, source): source
* Build command you used (if compiling from source): USE\_VULKAN=1 USE\_VULKAN\_SHADERC\_RUNTIME=1 USE\_VULKAN\_WRAPPER=0 python3 setup.py install
* Python version: 3.8
* CUDA/cuDNN version: 10.2
* GPU models and configuration: Titan V
* Any other relevant information: Used nvidia vulkan

$ vulkaninfo | grep "GPU id"

```
Presentable Surfaces:
=====================
GPU id : 0 (TITAN V):
GPU id : 1 (llvmpipe (LLVM 11.0.0, 256 bits)):
```

author: SS-JIA
text: As a sanity check, are you able to run other Vulkan programs on your machine? For example the Vulkan samples: <https://github.com/LunarG/VulkanSamples>
